title: Members of the Chemicals Management Plan Science Committee - Canada.ca
content:
## Members of the Chemicals Management Plan Science Committee
The Science Committee consists of 9 core members who serve an initial term of up to 3 years and may be re-appointed for a second term. A mechanism is also in place to include ad hoc members when additional expertise is required. Members of this committee serve as knowledgeable individuals in their own right and in the best interest of Canadians. They do not represent their employers, organizations, or professional affiliations.
Core membership of the committee is as follows:
- Dr. Miriam Diamond (co-chair) Biography Affiliations and interests
- Affiliations and interests
- Mr. Mike Rasenberg (co-chair) Biography Affiliations and interests
- Affiliations and interests
- Dr. Jon Arnot Biography Affiliations and interests
- Affiliations and interests
- Dr. Niladri Basu Biography Affiliations and interests
- Affiliations and interests
- Dr. Richard Becker Biography Affiliations and interests
- Affiliations and interests
- Dr. Weihsueh Chiu Biography Affiliations and interests
- Affiliations and interests
- Dr. Michelle Embry Biography Affiliations and interests
- Affiliations and interests
- Mr. Geoff Granville Biography Affiliations and interests
- Affiliations and interests
- Dr. Elaine Cohen Hubal Biography Affiliations and interests
- Affiliations and interests
The members of this committee were recruited through an open self-nomination process and selected by Environment and Climate Change Canada and Health Canada. Selection of members was based upon areas of expertise such as environmental/biological science, chemicals management framework, predictive tools and models, new approach methodologies, as well as knowledge of the chemicals industry. Priority was given to those with significant and recent relevant experience, and strong applicable academic and/or regulatory science credentials.
To be considered for appointment, applicants were required to:
- Complete an Affiliations and Interests Declaration form; and
- Sign a Confidentiality Agreement.
The Call for Representation for the Science Committee was closed on April 30, 2017.
